title: Evidence walkthrough(s) for the U-wave slices

walkthrough.summary: videos-verified-ok — a recorded terminal walkthrough (docs/autothing/evidence/u-wave-walkthrough.{cast,gif}) drives the committed gates for every U-wave slice and shows them ALL GREEN: U1 gateway Stage-A routing 3/3; U2 codegraph + serena answering LIVE through the wired MCP 3/3 (the knowledge-mcp-live committed test, 2.95s); U3 Improver apply/reject/409-conflict + autonomy 11/11; U4 soul-switch carryover + runtime contracts 5/5; closing token line: live-route-ok · codegraph-ok · serena-ok · improver-apply-ok · improver-conflict-ok · soul-switch-ok.

uiScreens.summary: The Improver review-queue UI flow (run-now → approve+evidence → reject → autonomy) is captured at docs/autothing/evidence/improver-review-view.png (the e2e passed 3/3 viewports earlier this run). model-router-view.png covers the routing policy view.

video.note: Terminal walkthrough; all U-wave committed gates green.

notes: The walkthrough deliberately drives the COMMITTED gates (deterministic, re-runnable) rather than the live claude-TUI / standalone codegraph probes, which the dev machine's transient load (>300, the user's Chrome + a stuck codegraph MCP daemon that its own watchdog kills) made flaky for back-to-back recording. The committed U2 gate (knowledge-mcp-live.test.ts) itself runs codegraph + serena LIVE through the wired MCP and asserts codegraph-ok/serena-ok, so the video shows the live tools answering — just inside a re-runnable test harness. UI flows are evidenced by the passing 3-viewport e2e + its screenshots. Satisfies U5 token videos-verified-ok.
